title: pyrola rotundifolia subsp. incarnata
intro: Pyrola rotundifolia subsp. incarnata, also known as red-berried wintergreen, is a plant species in the heath family that is native to mountainous regions in Europe. Its medicinal properties include the presence of methyl salicylate, which is similar to aspirin, making it a potential natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Pyrola rotundifolia subsp. incarnata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and berries have been used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for various herbal preparations.
